pro_brother.song_name: Everybody Wants To Rule The World

content:
Song: Everybody wants to rule the world
Artist: Tears for Fears
Composers: Roland Orzabal, 
           Ian Stanley and Chris Hughes
Album: Songs from the big chair

Tabbed by DJ   19th Sept 2018

This tab is an arrangement with only one acoustic
guitar in mind as an accompaniment and doesn't aim
to reflect the way the original song was played.

IMPORTANT NOTE:
The original backing track appears to have been
sped up, making the track sharp to very nearly a
whole half step up. 
You will need to tune all your strings up higher,
by approx. + 20 cents, to closer match the pitch of
the original recording.
To avoid retuning I recommend using a live concert
performance if you want to play along with the song

2022 live TV performance (Standard tuning):
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NZ-o1N2swMw

I would recommend using the voicings in the text
column numbers below.
Regarding the CHORD BOX DIAGRAMS please follow the
information at the bottom of the tab.

A/D   = xx0220   Gmaj7 = 320002    Bm      = x24432
G/D   = xx0003   A     = x02220    Cadd9   = x32030
Em9   = 020032   G     = 320033    A6      = x02222
F#m   = 244222   D     = xx0232    Gmaj7/D = xx0002

^  = Emphasise the beats

The instrumental break chords are just my own
suggestions, the record actually stays on the
D chord riff but with shifting bass notes added.

Please take a second to scroll down to the bottom of
the page and select a star rating for my tab, thanks

=====================================================
For the GUITAR chord box diagrams to match my
suggested choices you need to manually select the
following choice of options by clicking on the
forward/backward arrows below three of the diagrams.
   Em9         G           A6
<2 of 23>   <2 of 27>   <2 of 44>

The remaining chord boxes can be the default
first option and don't need any reselecting.
As long as you are logged into your account,
these choices will be saved automatically for every
time you view this tab
